subject: [Consul] Remove nodeport on UI
commit_message:
[Consul] Remove nodeport on UI

consul ui is on http mode.
As nobody really looks at it, let's remove the nodeport and set it as
ClusterIP.

Issue-ID: OJSI-168
Issue-ID: OJSI-202
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: If114dac59c4fc919a0ab879ef7c5c2478f6a16d2

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> @Sylvain
> Are you sure that azure jobs uses SKIP_LINT=TRUE? I've just checked it and on my latop it takes only:
> 
> real	0m36,985s
> user	0m45,942s
> sys	0m2,969s

well I strictly use the same "code" to send on Azure or Orange Openstack.
the whole preparation jobs takes ~20min on Openstack and ~42min on Azure and I don't know why.
I'll dig into it today

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> > Patch Set 2:
> > 
> > @Sylvain
> > Are you sure that azure jobs uses SKIP_LINT=TRUE? I've just checked it and on my latop it takes only:
> > 
> > real	0m36,985s
> > user	0m45,942s
> > sys	0m2,969s
> 
> well I strictly use the same "code" to send on Azure or Orange Openstack.
> the whole preparation jobs takes ~20min on Openstack and ~42min on Azure and I don't know why.
> I'll dig into it today

so ansilbe is launching "/bin/sh -c SKIP_LINT=TRUE /usr/bin/python" which launch "/usr/bin/python" which launch "make all" on Azure right now.
So it should be good no?
oom_redeploy

Patch Set 2:

Nope it's not;)

you need to SKIP_LINT=TRUE as an argument to make, it's not gonna work in a way you described (or at least it doesn't work form me)

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

But still it seems that this time it all passed correctly. All failures are pretty much the same that we observe in other reviews so let's merge this one.
Change has been successfully merged by Krzysztof Opasiak

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Nope it's not;)
> 
> you need to SKIP_LINT=TRUE as an argument to make, it's not gonna work in a way you described (or at least it doesn't work form me)

you mean make SKIP_LINT=TRUE all?
I'm pretty sure the way I do is working but still ;)

FYI, azure controllers have onbly 2 vCPUs (4vCPU on OpenStack) so maybe it's the issue. I've changed that and Azure 2 is in reinstallation process.
